127145.explanation: It is good to have a diverse composition in the executive board. However, this depends on more factors than just the male/female ratio. It also depends on specific knowledge and experience that administrators bring. Therefore, we disagree with the statement.

127146.explanation: We find it important to involve our residents in policy development and important issues. We often do this by starting a participation process. We do not exclude the option of a referendum.

127150.explanation: If there are initiatives from residents or entrepreneurs that can contribute to our ambitions in sustainability, they could receive financial support through subsidies. Examples include large batteries per neighborhood or street to store sustainable energy.

127151.explanation: We support economic developments that contribute to extra employment. We do not find it wise to prohibit the establishment of new distribution centers upfront.

127152.explanation: We believe the national government should limit the influx of asylum seekers. We do not think an asylum seeker center (azc) should be established in our municipality as an end goal.

127154.explanation: This suggests that our residents and entrepreneurs would also be obliged to replant a cut tree on their own land. This leads to extra rules and government interference, which we do not favor. We want a small government with fewer rules.

127157.explanation: If there is a need and broad support among our residents, the municipality could organize this.

127160.explanation: We favor good and accessible shops. As a government, we also have obligations under the UN Convention to make shops accessible to people with disabilities. We can imagine a financial scheme to support entrepreneurs financially in this.

127161.explanation: Yes, we are in favor of this within the applicable rules.

127162.explanation: Yes, this can already be done through Stichting Leergeld, and we support that.

127163.explanation: Yes, a strong local broadcaster is very important. We support this and will continue to do so.

127164.explanation: Yes, we agree with that. We find it important that children at primary and secondary school come into contact with music. This scheme already exists, and we support it.

127168.explanation: Safety is extremely important to us as VVD. That is why we have previously advocated for extra enforcement officers in our municipality. Maintaining a police station here is also very important.

127169.explanation: To counter nuisance and crime, we favor the use of camera surveillance, for example, in our shopping centers.

127174.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for tourists. We want to invest extra in our tourism and recreation sector and attract more tourists to our municipality. This is also good for shopping centers and other amenities. Converting holiday homes to permanent residences does not fit with this.

127175.explanation: We support building additional homes in Weststellingwerf. This aims to solve the housing shortage affecting many groups. Building in the villages but also outside village borders should be possible (within regulations).

127176.explanation: We believe people receiving social assistance benefits should give something back to society in return.

127178.explanation: We find it important that everyone can make ends meet and provide for themselves. We want as few people as possible to need assistance. Helping with poverty is part of our minimum policy. We do not want to abolish that, but work should primarily pay off.

127180.explanation: The municipality must take developments in electric cars into account. If more electric cars appear, more charging points will be needed. These should mostly be added while ensuring enough (regular) parking spaces remain.

127181.explanation: We are not in favor of mandating a 30 km/h speed limit everywhere in built-up areas. We can imagine that in some places within built-up areas 50 km/h is still allowed, such as on the Om Den Noort ring road in Wolvega.

127182.explanation: We find it important that care can primarily be provided. In an ideal world, freedom of choice for a care provider who fits one’s religion, culture, or conviction is good. But the most important thing is that care is provided.

127183.explanation: Price is important, but the quality of care is even more important. Youth care can be used more efficiently and thus more cheaply.

127186.explanation: We believe the revenue from tourist tax should be used for investments in the tourism sector. We are satisfied with the current system as it is.

127187.explanation: We support economic developments that contribute to more employment. For the future, it is good to focus on more business parks that can provide jobs and economic growth.

127190.explanation: We favor low charges for our residents and entrepreneurs. However, waste collection must be cost-covering. It simply cannot be free, not even for disposing of waste at the recycling center. Green waste is free and should remain so.

127195.explanation: We are not opposed to a new coffeeshop in principle. This must occur within rules and policies. A coffeeshop should not become a nuisance spot.

127198.explanation: We support building extra homes in Weststellingwerf to help solve the housing shortage affecting many groups. If renting out single rooms helps solve the housing shortage, we are not opposed to that, provided that there is good landlord management.

127199.explanation: If there is a parking problem in a locality, more parking spaces must be created.

127200.explanation: We have opposed new locations for gas extraction during the past period. This is also the municipality's standing policy.

127201.explanation: We already enforce this strictly. From our perspective, no stricter control than currently practiced is necessary.

127145.explanation: WB does not rely on fixed quotas but focuses on quality, experience, and local involvement. The program emphasizes reliability, expertise, and accountability of administrators. Equal opportunities are a given; a mandatory distribution does not fit the governance style advocated by WB.

127146.explanation: WB wants to involve residents early and seriously in important decisions. Although the word “referendum” is not central literally, an advisory referendum fits well with the principle that residents should have a say over significant matters in their living environment.

127150.explanation: WB supports local energy initiatives and cooperatives, provided they are feasible and affordable. Local generation with local benefits aligns with the principle of collaborating with residents instead of top-down policies.

127151.explanation: New distribution centers can provide additional employment and strengthen Weststellingwerf’s economy. For many residents, they offer accessible jobs close to home. Careful integration is needed to ensure that traffic, landscape, and livability are not disproportionately impacted.

127152.explanation: WB is very clear here: no large-scale asylum seekers’ center in Weststellingwerf. The capacity of villages and facilities is limited. However, there is attention for humanity and temporary, small-scale shelter in exceptional situations.

127154.explanation: Green policies and livability are important to WB. Trees contribute to biodiversity, climate adaptation, and a pleasant living environment. Compensation for tree cutting is logical.

127157.explanation: Weststellingwerfs Belang attaches great importance to safety, livability, and community spirit. A fireworks show organized by the municipality can contribute to a festive New Year's Eve and strengthen social cohesion.

127160.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Financial support helps entrepreneurs make buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

127161.explanation: Possibly through tailored solutions, but agricultural land must also be protected. Village expansion should be careful and limited in scale.

127162.explanation: Sport promotes health and social participation. Residents with low incomes should not be excluded.

127163.explanation: Local broadcasters provide nearby information and strengthen democracy and engagement. Extra support is given only if necessary and then incidentally.

127164.explanation: Music education stimulates creativity and development. Municipal support increases equal opportunities for children.

127168.explanation: Visible community officers increase safety and livability. Expanding enforcement is necessary.

127169.explanation: Human supervision and accountability have priority. Cameras can be supplementary but not standard.

127174.explanation: This disrupts the housing market and recreational provision. Functions must remain clear.

127175.explanation: Villages need space to grow (limited) so that they remain livable.

127176.explanation: Participation according to ability strengthens involvement and self-worth, provided it is meaningful and appropriate.

127178.explanation: More people need help due to rising costs. Broadening access prevents debts.

127180.explanation: Part of realistic sustainability and support for electric driving.

127181.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas increases traffic safety, especially for pedestrians, cyclists, children, and the elderly. It reduces accidents and creates a more pleasant living environment in villages and neighborhoods. This aligns with WB’s pursuit of safe and livable villages.

127182.explanation: Care must align with personal beliefs and backgrounds. This increases trust and effectiveness.

127183.explanation: Quality, proximity, and effectiveness are more important than the lowest price.

127186.explanation: Tourist tax generates funds that flow back to recreation, tourism, and facilities also used by visitors. In this way, tourists contribute to maintenance, livability, and sector promotion. Therefore, reduction is undesirable.

127187.explanation: Only with demonstrable need and appropriate integration into landscape and infrastructure.

127190.explanation: Good waste facilities are important, but free services are not always efficient. Efficient and affordable waste policies take priority. Easy waste disposal encourages recycling and prevents dumping.

127195.explanation: Preservation of peace, safety, and livability is paramount.

127198.explanation: Only through customized solutions to prevent nuisance and deterioration of living quality.

127199.explanation: Accessibility of localities and facilities remains important.

127200.explanation: WB opposes (expansion of) gas extraction due to risks and damage.

127201.explanation: Protection of youth and enforcement of rules is necessary.

127145.explanation: Strive for a good reflection of society. Not at the expense of quality.

127146.explanation: We want residents to have a significant voice in decisions affecting their neighborhood or that concern them directly. Initially, a referendum seems the right instrument. We prefer a citizens' assembly.

127150.explanation: We attach great importance to realizing the energy transition. As a party, we will support that course.

127151.explanation: We oppose urbanization of our municipality. We contribute to a sustainable society.

127152.explanation: We support enforcement of the distribution law.

127154.explanation: The municipality already has this policy. We think this is good policy.

127157.explanation: A nice alternative to setting off fireworks yourself. The question is whether this is realistic.

127160.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for this themselves.

127161.explanation: Allows - Red-for-Red scheme. Opportunity to convert old agricultural buildings into homes.

127162.explanation: Foundation Leergeld Werf (SLW) helps children from families with financial worries with sports club fees.

127163.explanation: Good media are essential in a democratic society. This can mean financial support.

127164.explanation: SLW helps children from families with financial worries with the costs of music lessons.

127168.explanation: Do more BOAs (special enforcement officers) really help? Is there enough police responding quickly to reports? Who pays for that?

127169.explanation: There is no reason for that in Weststellingwerf.

127174.explanation: We have no opinion on this.

127175.explanation: We support expanding the boundaries for housing development in the villages.

127176.explanation: We start from positive treatment and see what people need to participate.

127178.explanation: People must have sufficient income to participate. It is a form of civilization to offer help.

127180.explanation: We want to encourage electric cars, so more charging stations are needed.

127181.explanation: It is of great importance for traffic safety. Where possible, speed can be differentiated.

127182.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for that. And we wonder if this is realistic in a small municipality.

127183.explanation: The starting point must be professional prevention. This will lead to a reduction of youth care costs.

127186.explanation: Tourists use municipal facilities such as bike paths. The fee is reasonable.

127187.explanation: The municipality must have sufficient business space for small-scale and green enterprises.

127190.explanation: The polluter pays principle applies. This also applies to our residents.

127195.explanation: We are in favor of legalizing soft drugs. We are not against a new coffee shop.

127198.explanation: We have no position on this.

127199.explanation: We do not see the need for more parking spaces in the centers.

127200.explanation: This is already municipal policy. We will continue to resist in the future!

127201.explanation: Checking this is difficult. More education about alcohol use may help better.

127145.explanation: For the CDA, quality and suitability are paramount.

127146.explanation: Citizen participation is important, but elected representatives remain leading.

127150.explanation: Together with residents, generate sustainable energy and look into financial support.

127151.explanation: Economic development must be possible, provided it fits within the environment.

127152.explanation: Balanced reception policy with attention to support and integration.

127154.explanation: Stewardship and a green living environment are important for the CDA.

127157.explanation: Safety is important for the CDA, but not an explicit priority.

127161.explanation: Use agricultural land sparingly and this requires a tailored approach.

127162.explanation: Participation must be possible for everyone and may involve financial support.

127163.explanation: Local connection and provision of information for our residents is important.

127164.explanation: Culture and the development of children are important and may involve financial support.

127168.explanation: Safety is important, but not only repressive.

127174.explanation: As a solution to the housing shortage, but with conditions.

127175.explanation: Infill where possible, but expansion must also be possible.

127178.explanation: Helping more people who just fall outside the safety net.

127182.explanation: Freedom and respect for identity are important.

127187.explanation: Only if there is demonstrable need.

127195.explanation: From public order and livability perspectives.

127198.explanation: Only if livability is not under pressure.

127199.explanation: Depending on the core (villages) and the situation.

parties.name: SDW/PvdA
parties.fullName: Sociaal Duurzaam Weststellingerwerf/Partij van de Arbeid

127145.explanation: The quality of the officials comes first. But an equal distribution is a nice goal.

127146.explanation: The municipal council is perfectly capable of representing the opinions of residents on important issues. Councillors know what is happening in society and are able to decide in the general interest.

127150.explanation: Municipal support can be important during the startup phase.

127151.explanation: These take up too much scarce space and hardly create any employment.

127152.explanation: We believe that foreigners who have received a residence status from the Immigration and Naturalisation Service (IND) should also be able to find a safe house and home in Weststellingwerf. As for providing the number of places in asylum seeker centers (AZCs), Weststellingwerf will continue to meet the statutory task.

127154.explanation: In a green municipality, we must be careful with our trees.

127157.explanation: It would be nice if the municipal hospitality sector would organize that and the municipality facilitates it.

127160.explanation: This is the entrepreneurs' own responsibility. Moreover, the building regulations are leading on this matter.

127161.explanation: This leads to uncontrolled growth and further hardening of the rural area. For us, spatial quality comes first.

127162.explanation: In Weststellingwerf, people with a limited budget, including those on social assistance, can receive help with sports fees through the Adults Fund for Sport & Culture (for 18+) and Stichting Leergeld (for children under 18).

127163.explanation: The central government's contribution is usually sufficient. Extra support should be possible for special events.

127164.explanation: For music lessons with social assistance in Weststellingwerf, there are options via Stichting Leergeld (for children 0-18). Some parents are perfectly able to pay for the music lessons themselves.

127168.explanation: Residents often feel unsafe, especially in the evening. The presence of enforcement officers (BOAs) on the street can alleviate this feeling.

127169.explanation: We want camera surveillance only if it is necessary for the enforcement of safety and public order. Cameras must be functional.

127174.explanation: Holiday homes are not suitable as a permanent residence or address. Moreover, these homes are important for us as a recreation municipality where vacationers can stay.

127175.explanation: Our preference is to build within the existing village boundaries, but where that is not possible, building outside the existing boundaries can be a solution.

127176.explanation: In an ideal world, this would be possible, but not everyone is capable of this. We start from what someone can do. Participation is important: Together we do more!

127178.explanation: We are fully committed to combating poverty and debt. We will maintain the current poverty reduction package and expand it if possible.

127180.explanation: Work is currently underway to increase the number of charging points.

127181.explanation: This decision has already been made. We want the roads to be adapted quickly. 30 km/h within the built-up area is essential for traffic safety.

127182.explanation: It would be nice if this were possible, but for us, good care is paramount.

127183.explanation: Price is important, quality as well.

127186.explanation: This is currently at an acceptable level. It is a fair system. The tourist tax is a percentage of the accommodation turnover and not a fixed amount per overnight stay. The rate is 4%. This way, everyone pays according to the size of their business.

127187.explanation: The current supply is sufficient. Existing businesses should be able to expand so people can also work in our municipality in the future. Therefore, the sites may be expanded in the future.

127190.explanation: This is already the case for green waste and small chemical waste. For bulky household waste, payment will also be required in the future because disposal and processing are costly.

127198.explanation: This already happens. Whether it can be allowed depends on the building. Safety for residents comes first.

127199.explanation: There are currently enough parking spaces, but that may change in the future. The priority is that parking remains free in Weststellingwerf.

127200.explanation: This is already happening. Also for future developments, that will be our approach.

127201.explanation: Inspections are carried out regularly. We want to maintain that. Sports clubs must be convinced that alcohol use by minors is unacceptable.

127145.explanation: Quality, experience, and support must be leading, not quotas.

127146.explanation: There are currently no rules for a referendum. BBB wants clarity on this before starting a referendum.

127150.explanation: When residents generate energy together, the profits remain local and support grows.

127151.explanation: Weststellingwerf must be careful with its landscape and livability. New large distribution centers often do not fit with that.

127152.explanation: Weststellingwerf should not be disproportionately burdened. First support, distribution, and clear conditions.

127154.explanation: Green is important for nature, climate, and living environment. Replanting must therefore be the starting point.

127157.explanation: Only if this actually leads to less nuisance and damage.

127160.explanation: An accessible society is important. With support, entrepreneurs can better adapt their premises for everyone.

127161.explanation: BBB opposes building on agricultural land but supports placing a house for family on their own property.

127162.explanation: Sports and physical activity must be possible for everyone, including those with low incomes.

127163.explanation: Local media are important, but support must be used carefully and purposefully.

127164.explanation: Cultural education is valuable, but not everything needs to be organized by the municipality.

127168.explanation: More visible enforcement increases the feeling of safety and helps address nuisance in villages and neighborhoods.

127169.explanation: Camera surveillance can help, but must be targeted and privacy must be guaranteed. Lighting dark spots also enhances safety.

127174.explanation: This can help with housing shortages, but livability must remain leading.

127175.explanation: Building near existing villages is preferred, but sometimes expansion is needed to solve housing shortages.

127176.explanation: Participation according to ability is important. A reciprocal contribution can promote involvement and structure.

127178.explanation: By helping earlier you prevent bigger problems and debts.

127180.explanation: Charging stations are needed but must be well integrated without causing extra parking pressure.

127181.explanation: In residential areas, 30 km/h can be good for safety. On through roads, customization should remain possible.

127182.explanation: Everyone must be able to receive care that fits their background and beliefs.

127183.explanation: In youth care, quality must always weigh heavier than the lowest price.

127186.explanation: A lower tourist tax makes the municipality more attractive to visitors and entrepreneurs.

127187.explanation: There must be space for local entrepreneurs and employment.

127190.explanation: Encouraging waste separation is important, but costs must remain fairly distributed.

127195.explanation: A coffeeshop does not fit the scale and character of the municipality.

127198.explanation: Loose room rentals can put pressure on livability in villages and neighborhoods.

127199.explanation: Good accessibility is important for residents, shops, and associations.

127200.explanation: The safety of residents must come first. New gas extraction causes worries and unrest, especially without clear support and firm guarantees.

127201.explanation: Protecting young people from alcohol abuse is important.

127145.explanation: For Hart voor Weststellingwerf quality and local involvement matter, not gender. Officials should be chosen based on knowledge, experience, and dedication to our municipality, not on quotas. We do want to reduce the number of aldermen to 3, saving 125,000 euros per year.

127146.explanation: In major decisions directly affecting residents, they must be able to express their opinions. A referendum increases support and trust and ensures decisions truly reflect local sentiment.

127150.explanation: The municipality facilitates local energy initiatives but is cautious with subsidies. Financial support only if transparent, temporary, fair, and contributes to broad support.

127151.explanation: Large distribution centers do not fit with the rural character of Weststellingwerf. They offer little local benefit and cause extra traffic and land use.

127152.explanation: Weststellingwerf is a small municipality with limited facilities. A reception center has a large impact and does not fit here. Shelter must be organized responsibly and with support.

127154.explanation: Green is important for livability. Therefore, when a tree is cut down, a new one must be planted, preferably within the municipality. This keeps the greenery at a good level.

127157.explanation: A central fireworks show provides safety, togetherness, and less nuisance. It offers a festive alternative accessible to everyone in the municipality.

127160.explanation: Accessibility is important, but financial support must be considered case by case. The municipality is willing to cooperate but does not automatically provide subsidies without clear necessity.

127161.explanation: Building housing on agricultural land requires caution. Sometimes it fits, sometimes it doesn't. The municipality must assess each case to determine what is spatially and socially responsible.

127162.explanation: Sports are important for health and social contacts. People with low incomes must also be able to participate. The municipality helps where necessary to remove barriers.

127163.explanation: The local broadcaster is important, but extra structural funding is not a given. The municipality must handle public funds carefully.

127164.explanation: Music education is valuable, but not every child needs it. The municipality should support initiatives without subsidizing everything centrally.

127168.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and accountability. Additional community officers (boa's) allow for quicker interventions and more peace in neighborhoods and villages. We want boa's to receive more authority.

127169.explanation: Safety requires presence and contact, not more cameras. Camera surveillance undermines privacy and is not a solution for social problems.

127174.explanation: In certain cases, permanent residence can be a solution to housing shortages. This requires clear rules to prevent misuse.

127175.explanation: Housing shortages sometimes call for building beyond existing boundaries. This must be done carefully, with attention to landscape and need, but should not be ruled out in advance.

127176.explanation: Participating to the best of one's ability is important. Socially meaningful work increases engagement and self-worth, provided personal circumstances are considered.

127178.explanation: Help must reach those who really need it. The municipality must deliver customized solutions and look at individual situations, not just fixed limits.

127180.explanation: Electric driving is increasing. Sufficient charging points are needed, provided they are balanced and fit the local situation.

127181.explanation: Lower speed increases traffic safety and livability in residential areas. This requires a realistic and clear road design.

127182.explanation: Good care must be accessible to everyone. Extra selection based on religion or culture should not lead to fragmentation or exclusion.

127183.explanation: Quality must come first in youth care. Cheap care that doesn’t work helps no one and can worsen problems.

127186.explanation: Tourism is important for the local economy. A lower tourist tax makes Weststellingwerf more attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs.

127187.explanation: There is sufficient available space. New sites impact the landscape and are not always necessary. Existing space should be utilized first.

127190.explanation: Free disposal leads to higher costs for everyone. A fair contribution per user keeps the system affordable.

127195.explanation: A coffeeshop does not fit the character and scale of Weststellingwerf. The municipality should focus on peace, safety, and livability in the villages.

127198.explanation: Room rental can lead to nuisance and pressure on neighborhoods. The municipality must keep residential areas livable and prevent subdividing of homes.

127199.explanation: Parking pressure differs per locality. The municipality must assess each village’s needs without automatically creating extra parking spaces everywhere.

127200.explanation: Gas extraction carries risks for safety and the living environment. The municipality must clearly oppose it and stand up for residents and the landscape. We do not want new gas extraction and want to share in the revenues from existing gas extraction. This money should flow back to the community through an initiative fund.

127201.explanation: Responsible alcohol use is important. Associations have a role in this, with support and education from the municipality instead of just stricter controls.

127145.explanation: It is about the right person with the right qualifications for the job.

127146.explanation: LRW advocates openness and clarity. No meetings behind closed doors. Major decisions - for example about wind turbines, large construction projects, or reorganization - must always be able to be presented to citizens through a local referendum.

127150.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf stands for realistic environmental policy.

127151.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf wants a healthy business climate that provides employment.

127152.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf wants to first solve the housing problem for our own citizens.

127154.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes farmers, gardeners, citizens, and entrepreneurs are good landscape stewards. We also want fewer rules from the municipality.

127157.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf wants to promote livability and cohesion in our villages and oppose a centralized fireworks event.

127160.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes entrepreneurs can perfectly assess their customers’ needs themselves.

127161.explanation: LRW wants housing to be built for our own citizens first. New homes must be affordable and fit the villages. No mass construction or high-rise buildings, but small-scale projects that fit the landscape and identity of our municipality.

127162.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes sports connect people, keep us healthy, and strengthen the community. Local Rights Weststellingwerf wants everyone, young and old, to participate in sports and exercise - without it becoming unaffordable or bureaucratic.

127163.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf is proud of our Dutch culture and Stellingwerf identity. We stand for our language, traditions, and history - that is what unites us. The municipality should not erase or hide this identity, but actively protect and promote it.

127164.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes good education is the foundation of a strong society. The emphasis should be on language, arithmetic, history, technology, and citizenship again. Our children must learn what it means to be Dutch and be proud of their country and their municipality.

127168.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes safety is a basic condition for freedom. Everyone must feel safe - in the village, neighborhood, street, and sports club. Local Rights Weststellingwerf wants the municipality to be visibly present on the streets again, in cooperation with police and enforcement.

127169.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes nuisance caused by youth, drug use, or vandalism should not be glossed over in reports but resolved. LRW wants a tough approach against antisocial behavior, destruction, and intimidation. Safety is ensured with enforcement officers.

127174.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf wants the housing shortage to be resolved in the short term, requiring broader measures.

127175.explanation: LRW wants housing to be built for our own citizens first. We support plans for starter and senior homes.

127176.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes people receiving social assistance benefits don’t get them without reason.

127178.explanation: Local Rights Weststellingwerf believes municipal taxes must be lowered. Citizens already pay enough via energy bills, fuel, groceries, and national taxes. The municipality must help make life affordable again.

127180.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes that people who own an electric car should be able to charge it at a charging location/filling station just like a fuel-powered car, or arrange their own facilities.

127181.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes that the current speed limits are well regulated. If there are dangerous situations, they must be resolved.

127182.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes that care should be human. We believe that care should again focus on trust and proximity.

127183.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes that care must once again revolve around trust and proximity. Price should never be the reason.

127186.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf finds the current contribution very reasonable for what our municipality has to offer.

127187.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf wants priority to be given to housing for our youth, elderly, and citizens. We much prefer that outdated business parks are renovated.

127190.explanation: An important priority for LRW is the abolition of Diftar, the payment system where citizens pay per garbage bag or container. This system punishes families and low-income individuals and leads to littering and hassle. Environmentally conscious behavior is also penalized with higher costs.

127195.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes that we have sufficient venues.

127198.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes that a solution must quickly be found for the housing shortage.

127199.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes there are sufficient parking spaces. The only exception might be Noordwolde.

127200.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf fears soil subsidence and long-term damage without compensation. Unfortunately, we already have experience with this in our municipality.

127201.explanation: Lokaal Rechts Weststellingwerf believes that sports clubs are perfectly capable of supervising. Inspections are already taking place.

statements.title: The municipality should oppose gas extraction in Weststellingwerf.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Weststellingwerf should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Weststellingwerf.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Residents of Weststellingwerf should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Weststellingwerf moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should block the opening of a new coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
More business parks should be developed in Weststellingwerf.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should more strictly enforce rules on alcohol use in sports clubhouses by minors.
